codeReview.summary: Validation-only feature that ran the full test suite (929 tests) and visually verified the complete demo flow in QEMU. The worker also committed 10 files of previously-unstaged formatting/reordering changes left behind by prior polish workers. Detailed diff analysis confirms ALL 1140+/969- line changes are purely cosmetic: function reordering (truetype.rs, virtio-blk, virtio-gpu, virtio-input), struct reordering (virtio-input), constant reordering (kernel/main.rs, rasterizer.rs), blank line additions/removals (palette.rs, 9p, blk, gpu, text-editor), and re-indentation of an if-let block (init/main.rs). Zero functional changes. Verification was thorough: pixel-level screenshot analysis confirmed monochrome palette (R=G=B), smooth gradient, no banding, no flat line backgrounds, correct icon/document names, 18px font, local clock time, and clean serial output.

issues.description: The commit message says 'Committed 10 files of previously-unstaged working tree changes from prior polish workers.' A validation-only feature ideally should not commit code changes. However, since all changes are formatting-only (verified via diff) and the alternative was leaving uncommitted changes in the working tree, this is pragmatically acceptable. The commit attribution is slightly misleading since the formatting was done by other worker sessions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md test baseline says '921 tests must pass at all times (900 system + 21 prototype)' but the actual count is now 929 (908 system + 21 prototype). Prior polish workers added 8 tests that were never reflected in the baseline.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line 'Existing Test Baseline: 921 tests' vs. worker verification showing 908 system + 21 prototype = 929 total. Transcript confirms 'Total system tests: 908'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Multiple prior polish workers left formatting/reordering changes unstaged in the working tree, requiring the validation worker to commit them. This suggests either: (a) workers ran cargo fmt or a formatter without staging the results, or (b) workers manually reformatted code but forgot to include it in their commits. No convention exists for handling unstaged changes discovered by validation workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 54ba1ef includes 10 files with 1140 insertions / 969 deletions, all purely cosmetic (function reordering, blank line changes, re-indentation). These were found dirty in the working tree by the validation worker.

summary: PASS. Thorough validation-only feature. Worker ran 929 tests (all pass), built release with no warnings, booted QEMU, exercised the full text→image→text demo flow via Ctrl+Tab, performed pixel-level screenshot analysis verifying all polish criteria (no present logging, clean icons, Text/Image names, 18px font, local clock, smooth gradient, no banding, no flat lines, monochrome palette). Additionally committed 10 files of formatting-only changes left unstaged by prior workers — confirmed via diff analysis to contain zero functional changes.
